details: A multi-stage dropper masquerading as a corporate internal UI/logger toolkit (the keeneye[.]io domains are fabricated). Its postinstall (scripts/postinstall.js, heavily obfuscated) fingerprints the OS via platform(), builds a platform-specific remote URL, HTTP-fetches a payload, writes it to a temp file under the home directory, and spawns it as a child Node process with custom env vars. dist/index.js is a stub requiring a non-existent../src/index.js, so the package ships no real library code; the README's claim of telemetry to telemetry[.]keeneye[.]io is cover. An install-time download-and-execute implant. The obfuscated postinstall.js (javascript-obfuscator output) downloads a platform-specific payload from a remote URL and spawns process.execPath to execute it. The package is a stub: dist/index.js (76B) re-exports a non-existent../src/index.js, and the README version history is fabricated. Ships a multi-stage downloader in scripts/postinstall.js (heavily obfuscated). It determines the OS platform via os.platform(), fetches a platform-specific payload from a remote URL over http/https, writes it to ~/.keeneye-uikit, then spawns process.execPath to execute it. The main entry dist/index.js (76 bytes) requires a non-existent../src/index.js so there is no real logger functionality, and the README contains a fake changelog.
